methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
EdgeRunner AI is a Series A defense & security company founded by Tyler Xuan Saltsman and Colton Malkerson that has raised $17.5M to date. The valuation analysis incorporates eight same-sector private peers at Series A/B stage alongside two public comparables, providing a foundation for triangulation across different methodologies.

Two valuation approaches were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using the $17.5M total raised as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a $35M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the eight-company comp set yields a $1.0B implied valuation, with individual peer values ranging from $125M (low quartile) to $3.0B (Dream Security at the high end). The peer sample includes Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all valued at $1.0B–$3.0B in Series B.

Triangulation of these methods produces a valuation range of $30M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $518M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression suggests a more conservative $35M valuation anchored only to capital deployed, while peer comparables suggest substantially higher value based on market precedent for frontier defense AI. Key uncertainties include EdgeRunner's revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set. Revenue data and product maturity metrics would significantly narrow this range.
